contract.name: v2/autocomplete/accounts/aid.md
contract.description: Autocomplete lookup for Treasury agency identifiers based on TAS component filters

filters.description: Filter bag keyed by TAS component names (aid, ata, bpoa, epoa, a, main, sub). Only object payloads drive filtering; anything else behaves as if filters were omitted.
filters.constraints: Optional. Object members accept strings or null. aid uses prefix match on three-digit codes; other keys require exact matches. Empty strings raise a 422, but whitespace-only values are treated as empty and bypass filtering.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return; accepts integers or digit-only strings.
limit.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 10. Accepts 0-500 inclusive. Digit strings are coerced. Decimal or null values return 400 JSON errors, while negative integers yield an HTML 500.

results.description: Sorted ascending by aid. Each entry contains the agency identifier, display name, and optional abbreviation.

aid.description: Three-digit agency identifier returned as a string.

body.detail: Invalid value in 'limit'. '5.5' is not a valid type (integer)

quirks: Filters may be omitted, null, or any non-object value; anything except an object is ignored and the request returns the default unfiltered results.
Aid strings are trimmed; whitespace-only values bypass validation and behave like no filter.
Limit must be between 0 and 500, but digit strings are coerced. Negative integers trigger an HTML 500 instead of JSON.
Unknown filter keys and extra top-level parameters (for example, page) are silently ignored.
risks: Clients that omit filters unintentionally may receive broad unfiltered datasets because malformed payloads are silently accepted.
Leaving limit at its default 10 can truncate autocomplete choices; callers must raise it (<=500) to see the full universe.
Negative limit values cause a 500 HTML response, which breaks consumers expecting JSON errors.
gaps: Could not confirm how null component filters behave when matching data actually contains null component values.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"aid":"005","agency_name":"Government Accountability Office","agency_abbreviation":"GAO"},{"aid":"009","agency_name":"Legislative Branch Boards and Commissions","agency_abbreviation":"LEG ORGS"},{"aid":"010","agency_name":"The Judicial Branch","agency_abbreviation":"JUD BRANCH"},...]}

probes.notes: filters object allowed empty; default limit returned first 10 results

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"aid":"020","agency_name":"Department of the Treasury","agency_abbreviation":"TREAS"},{"aid":"021","agency_name":"Department of the Army","agency_abbreviation":null},{"aid":"024","agency_name":"Office of Personnel Management","agency_abbreviation":"OPM"}]}

probes.notes: aid prefix match respects provided limit
empty string for aid rejected with 422

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'limit'. '5.5' is not a valid type (integer)"}
{"results":[{"aid":"005","agency_name":"Government Accountability Office"},{"aid":"009","agency_name":"Legislative Branch Boards and Commissions"},{"aid":"010","agency_name":"The Judicial Branch"}]}

probes.notes: filters null accepted and falls back to unfiltered

mismatches: Docs state the filters object is required, but empty or missing filters succeed.
gaps: Could not confirm how null component filters behave when matching data actually contains null component values.
risks: Clients that omit filters unintentionally may receive broad unfiltered datasets because malformed payloads are silently accepted.
Leaving limit at its default 10 can truncate autocomplete choices; callers must raise it (<=500) to see the full universe.
Negative limit values cause a 500 HTML response, which breaks consumers expecting JSON errors.
